payload.title: It is such a fascinating quirk of history--the sort of thing that is not at all structural but is entirely contingent--t
payload.text:
It is such a fascinating quirk of history--the sort of thing that is not at all structural but is entirely contingent--that OpenAI and Anthropic were both founded during the heyday of "stakeholder capitalism" as a theory that would replace "shareholder capitalism."

The idea was

quotedTweet.text: This seems under-discussed: both Anthropic and OpenAI are structured as "public benefit corporations", a form of corporate governance that didn't even exist until 2010. It was designed with a political purpose, to reject, even replace shareholder primacy, the Milton Friedman
